steps.title: Use the graph intercepts
steps.content: The graph gives two clean intercepts: $(90,0)$ and $(0,35)$. A standard-form budget equation has the form $$ax+by=630,$$ where the intercepts reveal the two unit prices.

steps.title: Find the coefficients from the intercepts
steps.content: At the $x$-intercept, $y=0$, so $$90a=630 \Rightarrow a=7.$$ At the $y$-intercept, $x=0$, so $$35b=630 \Rightarrow b=18.$$ Therefore the equation is $$7x+18y=630.$$

steps.title: Match the answer
steps.content: Choice B — $7x+18y=630$ is $7x+18y=630$. The other standard-form choice swaps the costs, and the slope-intercept choices have positive slope, which does not match a fixed-budget line. Choice B — $7x+18y=630$.
